data.body:
Implemented on main (commit cee1fe98).

New rebar._store.hlc.next_tick(tracker, ticket_id) = max(per-clone cache, the
TARGET ticket's witnessed max event-prefix, physical_now()) + 1, RMW under a
dedicated local .rebar/hlc.lock (never held across the store write lock). The
.rebar/hlc.state cache is gitignored + rebuildable (correctness re-derived from the
durable log; a missing/stale/raced cache is still correct). Behind REBAR_HLC
(default-on; REBAR_HLC=0 kill-switch -> raw physical_now(), today's behavior). The
enabled RMW is best-effort: any error falls back to physical_now() so a write never
fails on the clock. physical_now() honors REBAR_HLC_NOW (the injectable test source).

Threaded through every live timestamp seam (9 sites, not the epic's "four"):
_seam.append_event, txn (transition STATUS + claim STATUS/EDIT), compact (SNAPSHOT),
delete (UNLINK + STATUS/ARCHIVED), graph._links (LINK), reconciler inbound_translate.

Ordering now compares the filename prefix AS AN INTEGER (reducer._sort.prefix_ts,
shared by graph/_links, _commands/unlink, _commands/txn) so legacy 19-digit and HLC
names form one global order regardless of width; UUID tiebreak kept for exact-equal
prefixes (STATUS forks still UUID-resolved). SCHEMA_VERSION -> 2; event-schema.md
updated. Same 19-digit width (to ~2286), so older string-comparing clones still order
correctly.

Tests — all green:
  - default suite 2085 passed + integration 20 passed with HLC DEFAULT-ON (no
    regression); kill-switch path REBAR_HLC=0 also green (integration 19 + core 281).
  - NEW skewed-clock EDIT causality convergence (tests/integration/
    test_concurrency_regression.py): B's edit made AFTER observing A's edit but on a
    far-SLOWER injected clock still wins on both clones (raw time_ns would clobber
    it); asserts B's prefix strictly exceeds the witnessed A prefix.
  - tests/unit/test_sort_prefix.py (integer width-comparator) + tests/unit/test_hlc.py
    (kill-switch, injection, strict monotonicity, per-ticket witness incl. no-cache).
  - ruff + ruff format + mypy (library core, 32 files) clean.

DEVIATIONS (codebase moved since the epic): (1) the epic said "four live seams" —
there are 9 live time_ns() event-stamp sites; all threaded. (2) The bash
`python3 -m rebar._store.hlc next` path is moot — ticket-compact.sh was already
ported to compact.py by the bash->Python migration, so no module CLI was added.
Per the plan's "default-on REBAR_HLC with kill-switch", HLC ships default-on.
